description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a wooden fuselage and two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked one above the other, connected by black struts. The wings and tail surfaces are painted in a warm brown color with green stripes, and the nose features a prominent wooden propeller and exposed engine cylinders. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windshield visible. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer with matching color patterns. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, likely used for training or reconnaissance.
description: A vintage wooden biplane with dual stacked wings, exposed engine, and open cockpit, painted in brown with green stripes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden propeller, exposed engine cylinders, and the stacked biplane wings with green stripes.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the open cockpit, and the tail section with its single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, including the horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the fixed landing gear.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the propeller, engine, and the full profile of the biplane's wings and tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or noticeable dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with exposed cylinders and a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located forward of the fuselage, just behind the upper wing.
